methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing limestone samples from Blue Hole and Devil's Den for analysis, including drying, crushing, sieving, and preparing thin sections for species-level identification.

steps.description: Samples were taken at approximately 1.5 m (5 ft) intervals measured from the air-water interface by scuba diving.
steps.searchable_summary: collect limestone samples at specified intervals using scuba diving

steps.description: Once collected, samples were dried, crushed and sieved through a series of size fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: dry, crush, and sieve limestone samples

steps.description: Foraminifera were then picked using a paintbrush under a stereoscope microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: pick foraminifera from samples using a stereoscope microscope

steps.description: Oriented sections of the LBF from each depth were then prepared as both equatorial and axial sections for species-level identification.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare oriented sections for species-level identification

steps.description: A total of ∼ 350 thin sections were utilized in the study, including both the original thesis slides prepared by James Floyd and additional slides prepared by Laura J. Cotton.
